generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance SINUMERIK840DController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. CNC controller enabling separate axis control (position, speed, or acceleration) and generating motor current (IM) output. Derives motor torque (τM) from current using manufacturer-provided motor constants."
]

instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor driving the first axis of MABI MAX 100 industrial robot, operating within [-90°, 90°] range and achieving maximum angular velocity of |q̇_max| = 75°/s."
]

instance GearWheelTransmission : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Gearwheel transmission component transforming rotational energy between motor and cycloidal gearbox within the first axis drive train."
]

instance CycloidalGearbox : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gearbox providing high torque reduction and precision motion control for the first axis, influencing output torque (τG) and system dynamics."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary position encoder mounted directly on the permanent magnet synchronous motor, providing real-time rotational position data (rM) for motion control."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorEncoderData
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary position encoder positioned behind the gearbox system, providing output angle (q) and enabling backlash analysis through dual encoder arrangement."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondaryEncoderData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorEncoderData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance SecondaryEncoderData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
]

instance DerivedDynamicsData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder, SecondaryEncoder
    base:desc "Computed dynamics data including torque (τ), angular velocity (ω), and acceleration (α) derived from sensor inputs using manufacturer-provided dynamics models."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLBlockDefinitionDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (BDD) describing structural composition of first axis components (motor, gearwheel, cycloidal gearbox) and their rotational/energy flows."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingTool
]

instance SysMLInternalBlockDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram (IBD) illustrating behavioral dynamics of motor-gearbox system including friction modeling components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingTool
]

instance AssetAdministrationShell : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Asset Administration Shell (AAS) providing semantic interoperability and metadata management for digital twin components, including system properties and data trace origins."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AASManagementTool
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Executable Coulomb-Viscous friction model implemented in MATLAB Simulink for simulating static and kinetic friction effects in the drive train."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulation
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Executable Stribeck friction model implemented in MATLAB Simulink accounting for stiction and breakaway behavior in the gearbox system."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulation
]

instance MotorOutputSimulation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Component-specific simulation model for motor output dynamics (Simulation_M) used to predict friction-influenced rotational behavior."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulation
]

instance GearWheelOutputSimulation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Component-specific simulation model for gearwheel output dynamics (Simulation_GW) analyzing torque transmission characteristics."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulation
]

instance CycloidalGearboxSimulation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Component-specific simulation model for cycloidal gearbox output dynamics (Simulation_GB) modeling high-precision motion characteristics."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulation
]

instance IdealGearboxModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Base simulation model representing ideal gearbox behavior without friction effects for comparative analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulation
]

instance MeasuredMotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw motor current (IM) data streamed via MQTT from SINUMERIK 840D controller to the digital twin."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorEncoderData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataCalculationEngine
]

instance MeasuredOutputAngleData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time output angle (q) data from secondary encoder transmitted via MQTT for position analysis and backlash compensation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryEncoderData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataCalculationEngine
]

instance DerivedTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Computed torque (τ) data derived from current and angle measurements using manufacturer-provided dynamics models."
    DTDFVocab:fromData DerivedDynamicsData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisEngine
]

instance DerivedAngularVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Computed angular velocity (ω) data derived from position encoder readings and time synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData DerivedDynamicsData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlStrategyRefinementService
]

instance DerivedAccelerationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Computed acceleration (α) data derived from velocity and position data for dynamic response analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData DerivedDynamicsData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo OperationalOptimizationService
]

instance SimulatedMotorOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Friction-influenced simulated motor output (δrM) generated by component-specific simulation models for comparison with measured data."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulatedDataOutput
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelValidationEngine
]

instance SimulatedGearWheelOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Friction-influenced simulated gearwheel output (δrG) generated by component-specific simulation models for torque analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulatedDataOutput
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelValidationEngine
]

instance SimulatedSystemOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Friction-influenced simulated system output (δr_out) representing complete drive train dynamics for validation purposes."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulatedDataOutput
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelValidationEngine
]

instance SimulatedDataOutput : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated simulated data output from all component-specific models used for comparison with measured data traces."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorOutputSimulation, GearWheelOutputSimulation, CycloidalGearboxSimulation
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MQTTProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol enabling real-time streaming of sensor data (IM, q) from physical system to digital twin with low-bandwidth requirements."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTransmissionService
]

instance SysMLModelingTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML modeling tool supporting Block Definition Diagrams and Internal Block Diagrams for structural and behavioral modeling of the drive train."
    DTDFVocab:enables StructuralModelingService, BehavioralModelingService
]

instance AASManagementTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Asset Administration Shell management tool providing semantic interoperability and metadata management for digital twin components."
    DTDFVocab:enables MetadataManagementService
]

instance SimulinkSimulation : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink environment executing friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck) and component-specific simulations for dynamic analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionIdentificationService, ControlStrategyRefinementService
]

instance DataCalculationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Engine for computing derived quantities (τ, ω, α) from raw sensor inputs using manufacturer-provided dynamics models."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAggregationService
]

instance FrictionAnalysisEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Multi-physics aware engine continuously identifying joint friction (static, kinetic, viscous) using white-box and black-box models."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionIdentificationService, DiagnosticAlertService
]

instance ModelValidationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Engine quantifying model fidelity by comparing simulated vs. measured trajectories and calculating % error reduction."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelValidationFeedbackService
]

instance DashboardVisualization : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization tool displaying simulated vs. measured data (torque, angular velocity) for diagnostic and decision-making processes."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataTransmissionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service responsible for real-time streaming of sensor data (IM, q) from physical system to digital twin via MQTT protocol."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataAggregationInsight
]

instance StructuralModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service creating SysML Block Definition Diagrams describing structural composition of first axis components and their energy flows."
    DTDFVocab:provides AssetModelingInsight
]

instance BehavioralModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service creating SysML Internal Block Diagrams illustrating behavioral dynamics including friction modeling components."
    DTDFVocab:provides DynamicsModelingInsight
]

instance MetadataManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service managing Asset Administration Shells for semantic interoperability and data trace provenance tracking."
    DTDFVocab:provides MetadataTrackingInsight
]

instance FrictionIdentificationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous service identifying joint friction (static, kinetic, viscous) using white-box and black-box models for predictive maintenance."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionParameterUpdateAction, DiagnosticAlertInsight
]

instance DataAggregationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service integrating operational data, live measurements, and simulation outputs into unified digital shadow for visualization."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalOptimizationInsight
]

instance ControlStrategyRefinementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service indirectly informing closed-loop control algorithms by providing corrected dynamics models for friction compensation."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlAdjustmentAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization service comparing simulated vs. measured data (torque, angular velocity) for diagnostic purposes."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualDiagnosticInsight
]

instance ModelValidationFeedbackService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service quantifying model fidelity through % error reduction between simulated and measured trajectories for iterative improvement."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelFidelityInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionParameterUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Real-time automatic updates to white-box and black-box friction models based on deviations between simulated and measured outputs."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DiagnosticAlertInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of anomalies such as sudden friction spikes for predictive maintenance (e.g., gearbox wear detection)."
]

instance DiagnosticAlertService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing diagnostic alerts for anomalies in system behavior."
    DTDFVocab:provides DiagnosticAlertInsight
]

instance ControlAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Indirect closed-loop control adjustments enabled by corrected dynamics models providing torque adjustments for friction compensation."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance OperationalOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing suggestions for tooling changes or speed adjustments to minimize friction-induced inaccuracies."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalOptimizationInsight
]

instance OperationalOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Suggestions for tooling changes or speed adjustments to minimize friction-induced inaccuracies in machining operations."
]

instance ModelFidelityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantification of model fidelity through % error reduction in simulated vs. measured trajectories for iterative improvement."
]

instance DataAggregationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Unified digital shadow integrating operational data, live measurements, and simulation outputs for comprehensive analysis."
]

instance AssetModelingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structural modeling insights from SysML Block Definition Diagrams describing first axis components and their energy flows."
]

instance DynamicsModelingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Behavioral modeling insights from SysML Internal Block Diagrams illustrating friction-influenced dynamics."
]

instance MetadataTrackingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Tracking of data provenance and semantic metadata through Asset Administration Shell management."
]

instance VisualDiagnosticInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time visual comparison of simulated vs. measured data enabling diagnostic decision-making processes."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The DT operates on a **real-time, continuous time scale**, synchronized with the physical system's operational dynamics. Data is processed at **high-frequency intervals** (not explicitly quantified but implied by MQTT streaming and sensor updates). The **twinning synchronization** occurs at the granularity of **individual data points** (e.g., torque/velocity measurements per time step) to enable **dynamic friction model updates** during robot operation."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is orchestrated as a **purpose-specific, modular pipeline** integrating:
   1. **Physical Layer**: MABI MAX 100 robot (first axis) with **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl.** controller and **dual encoders**.
   2. **Virtual Layer**:
      - **Data Processing**: Simulation components (Simulation_M, Simulation_GW, Simulation_GB) consuming input traces (e.g., I_M) and producing friction-adjusted outputs (ˆr_M, ˆr_G, ˆr_out).
      - **Model Layer**: SysML structural models, friction models (Simulink), and AAS for metadata.
      - **Dashboard**: Visualizes **measured vs. simulated data traces** (e.g., torque/velocity deviations) to enable **diagnostic insights**.
   3. **Interaction Layer**: One-way data flow from PT (MQTT) to DT, with **no closed-loop feedback** to the physical system. The constellation is designed for **continuous, real-time friction analysis** to improve motion accuracy."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT covers the **as-operated phase** of the physical system's lifecycle, focusing on **real-time monitoring and adaptive modeling** during the robot's operational deployment. It does not explicitly address **as-designed (ideal) or as-manufactured** representations, though the **SysML structural models** (e.g., Block Definition Diagrams) provide a foundation for **engineering-level abstractions**. The DT is designed for **continuous improvement** of friction models over time, aligning with the **operational phase** of the robot's lifecycle."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
   - **Model Verification**:
     - **SysML structural models** (BDDs/IBDs) validate the **physical composition** of the first axis (motor, gearboxes).
     - **Simulation models** (Simulink) are verified against **manufacturer-provided dynamics parameters** and **experimental friction data**.
   - **Validation Mechanisms**:
     - **Comparison of simulated vs. measured data traces** (e.g., ˆr_M vs. r_M) to quantify **friction-induced errors**.
     - **Uncertainty quantification**: Backlash and positional uncertainty are mitigated via **dual encoder arrangement** (motor + secondary encoder).
     - **Parameter sensitivity analysis**: Friction models (e.g., Coulomb-Viscous) are tuned using **real-time operational data** to adapt to wear, load, or temperature variations.
   - **Error Sources**:
     - **Sensor noise** in encoder measurements.
     - **Model approximation errors** in friction simulations (e.g., simplifications in Stribeck/Dahl models).
     - **Dynamic loading effects** (e.g., tool changes) not fully captured in static friction models."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between PT and DT is established via:
   - **Network Protocol**: **MQTT** for **real-time, low-latency data streaming** of sensor inputs (I_M, q) from the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. controller.
   - **Data Format**: Time-series arrays of **floating-point values** (torque τ, angular velocity ω) with **metadata** (source, timestamp).
   - **Synchronization**: **Event-triggered** (e.g., sensor updates) or **time-triggered** (implicit via MQTT QoS) to ensure alignment between physical and virtual states.
   - **Architecture**: **Client-server model**, where the PT (robot) acts as the **data provider**, and the DT (Simulink/processing components) acts as the **consumer**."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is hosted and deployed in a **software-defined environment** integrating:
   - **Simulation Platform**: **MathWorks MATLAB Simulink** for executing friction models and data processing.
   - **Model Repository**: SysML models (BDDs/IBDs) stored in a **model-based engineering toolchain** (unspecified vendor, but compatible with SysML standards).
   - **Dashboard Environment**: A **visualization tool** (unspecified) for real-time comparison of **measured vs. simulated data traces**, enabling **diagnostic and decision-making support**.
   - **Deployment Architecture**: **Centralized processing** on a **local or cloud-based server** (not specified) to handle real-time data ingestion and simulation."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT adheres to the following standards:
   - **Modeling**:
     - **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)** for structural and behavioral modeling (SysML Block Definition Diagrams, Internal Block Diagrams).
     - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** for semantic interoperability (part of the **Industry 4.0 reference architecture**).
   - **Data Exchange**:
     - **MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport)** for real-time data streaming (standardized protocol for IoT/industrial applications).
   - **Simulation**:
     - **MathWorks Simulink** (compatible with **Modelica** and other simulation standards) for executable friction models.
   - **Digital Twin Methodology**:
     - **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)** as defined in [15] and [38], which aligns with **ISO/IEC 42022** (Digital Twin standards) for **data traceability and processing lineage**."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The documents do not explicitly address **cybersecurity risks** (e.g., MQTT vulnerabilities, unauthorized access to friction models) or **safety considerations** (e.g., remote operation hazards). However, the following implicit safeguards are noted:
   - **Passive Digital Shadow**: The DT **does not execute autonomous actions** on the PT, reducing **accident risks** from remote control.
   - **Data Isolation**: Friction models and simulation outputs are **localized** to the DT environment, minimizing exposure to external threats.
   - **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl.**: The controller likely includes **industrial-grade security** (e.g., firewalls, access controls) for sensor data transmission.
   - **No Closed-Loop Feedback**: Absence of **virtual-to-physical actuation** eliminates risks of **unstable control loops** or **physical damage** from DT-generated commands."
]
